name: Thasos
intro: Thasos is the northernmost Greek island — closer to Bulgaria than to Athens — and it looks nothing like the Cyclades. Dense pine and plane forests cover the mountains, rivers feed marble quarries, and the east coast has long white-sand beaches. In antiquity, Thasos was famously wealthy from its gold and marble; today the marble is still exported, and the ancient Agora in the main town is one of the most complete in Greece.
subtitle: Northern forests, marble beaches, and ancient Agora

itinerary.title: 3-day Itinerary for Thasos
itinerary.subtitle: Northern forests, marble beaches, and ancient Agora

stops.name: Thasos Town (Limenas)
stops.desc: Main port and capital. The old town sits on top of the ancient city — you'll see ancient ruins in people's gardens.

stops.name: Ancient Agora
stops.desc: One of the best-preserved Agoras in Greece — paved Roman plazas, stoas, Hellenistic temples. Right next to the archaeological museum.

stops.name: Ancient Theatre of Thasos
stops.desc: 15-min climb through pine forest to the 4th-century BC theatre carved into the hillside. Still used for summer performances.

stops.name: Lunch — Simi Restaurant
stops.desc: Elegant waterfront spot with fresh fish and the best Thasian wine list.

stops.name: Chrysi Ammoudia (Golden Beach)
stops.desc: Long sandy east-coast beach. Pine forest runs right to the sand.

stops.name: Panagia Village
stops.desc: Traditional stone-built village with slate roofs and a spring running through the main square. Old-world Thasos.

stops.name: Potamia
stops.desc: Home of sculptor Polygnotos Vagis — small museum in the village celebrating his work.

stops.name: Aliki Archaeological Site
stops.desc: Ancient marble quarry on the southeast coast. Twin harbors, 7th-century BC temples, quarry cuts visible in the cliff face.

stops.name: Lunch — Armenaki
stops.desc: Taverna in the Aliki double bay. Fresh catch, grilled over charcoal.

stops.name: Theologos
stops.desc: Old capital of Thasos, high in the mountains. Traditional stone houses, best preserved village on the island.

stops.name: Limenaria
stops.desc: Second-largest town on Thasos. Working fishing port on the southwest coast.

stops.name: Giola Lagoon
stops.desc: Natural rock pool carved by the sea — a cliff-enclosed pool you dive into from 3 meters. Must-see on Thasos.

stops.name: Potos
stops.desc: Lively resort village with a long sandy beach and dozens of tavernas on the promenade.

stops.name: Marble Beach (Saliara)
stops.desc: Reached by 4x4 or boat. White marble pebbles and turquoise water — the most photogenic beach on Thasos.

beaches.name: Marble Beach (Saliara)
beaches.desc: Marble quarry remnants create this almost otherworldly white beach. Reached by 4x4 track or boat.

beaches.name: Giola Lagoon
beaches.desc: Natural pool carved from the cliff by the sea. Swim, dive, climb the rock walls.

beaches.name: Chrysi Ammoudia
beaches.desc: Thasos' biggest and most popular beach. Long stretch backed by pine forest.

beaches.name: Paradise Beach
beaches.desc: Quieter east-coast beach tucked into a cove. Turquoise water, pine shade.
